text: Databases help us store and organise data efficiently. The two main types are SQL and NoSQL. SQL databases, like MySQL and PostgreSQL, use structured tables with rows and columns. You can run queries using Structured Query Language (SQL) to retrieve or update specific information quickly. Data modeling in SQL involves designing tables and defining relationships between them. Indexes are special lookup tables that speed up searches by providing quick paths to data. NoSQL databases, such as MongoDB, are more flexible. They store data in documents or key-value pairs, making them ideal for unstructured or rapidly changing data. Choosing between SQL and NoSQL depends on your project’s needs for structure, scalability, and performance.
